description: SDK improvements: add setSessionStorage, add deleteJSON/patchJSON/putJSON, add typed waitForSelector generic, strengthen ResourceDefinition schema connection, fix prompt type erasure, fix getPageGlobal branded type, add hasEverHadPlugins reset confirmation.

userStories.title: Add setSessionStorage utility
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want a setSessionStorage utility to match the existing get/remove/set pattern for localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: It wraps sessionStorage.setItem with try-catch for SecurityError/QuotaExceededError (same pattern as setLocalStorage)

userStories.title: Add deleteJSON, patchJSON, putJSON fetch utilities
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want fetch utilities for common HTTP methods beyond GET and POST.
acceptanceCriteria: deleteJSON<T>(url, init?, schema?) is exported — sends DELETE request, parses JSON response
patchJSON<T>(url, body, init?, schema?) is exported — sends PATCH with JSON body, parses JSON response
putJSON<T>(url, body, init?, schema?) is exported — sends PUT with JSON body, parses JSON response
All three support optional Zod schema validation (same overload pattern as fetchJSON/postJSON)
All three use fetchFromPage internally (inheriting session cookies, timeout, error handling)

userStories.title: Add generic type parameter to waitForSelector
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want waitForSelector to return a typed element so I don't need manual casting.
acceptanceCriteria: waitForSelector<T extends Element = Element>(selector, opts?) returns Promise<T>
Calling waitForSelector('.foo') without generic returns Promise<Element> (backward compatible)
The runtime behavior is unchanged — only the type signature changes
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/plugin-sdk/src/dom.ts line 24. Change from:
```typescript
export const waitForSelector = (selector: string, opts?: WaitForSelectorOptions): Promise<Element> => {
```
To:
```typescript
export const waitForSelector = <T extends Element = Element>(selector: string, opts?: WaitForSelectorOptions): Promise<T> => {
```

Inside the function, the `document.querySelector(selector)` calls return `Element | null`. Cast the resolved value:
- Line where existing element is found: `resolve(existing as T);`
- Line where observer finds element: `resolve(el as T);`

This is a type-only change — the runtime behavior is identical.

userStories.title: Strengthen ResourceDefinition schema connection to read() return type
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the ResourceDefinition schema to provide a type-level connection to what read() returns, not just be decorative.
acceptanceCriteria: When a Zod schema is provided to defineResource, the read() function's return type for ResourceContent.text is guided by the schema type
At minimum, the schema type is preserved as a generic parameter on ResourceDefinition so downstream code can reference it
The JSDoc clearly states that runtime validation is NOT performed — this is a type-level contract only
Existing resources without schemas continue to work

userStories.title: Fix prompt type erasure in prompts array
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want typed prompt render functions to retain their types when stored in the prompts array.
acceptanceCriteria: definePrompt with args schema returns a type that preserves the schema type information
The OpenTabsPlugin.prompts array accepts typed prompt definitions without losing type info
At minimum, the render function's args parameter remains typed at the call site
Existing untyped prompts continue to work
userStories.notes:
The type erasure happens because OpenTabsPlugin.prompts is typed as `PromptDefinition[]` which erases the generic TArgs parameter.

The fundamental issue: TypeScript can't maintain heterogeneous generic types in an array. A `PromptDefinition<z.ZodObject<{name: z.ZodString}>>` stored in `PromptDefinition[]` becomes plain `PromptDefinition`.

This is the same pattern as `ToolDefinition[]` — each tool's TInput/TOutput is erased in the array. The type safety exists at the `defineTool`/`definePrompt` call site.

The best fix: ensure the type safety at the definition site is robust (which it already is via `TypedPromptConfig<TArgs>`) and document that the array erases types (which is expected TypeScript behavior). The render function IS typed when you call `definePrompt({ args: z.object({...}), render(args) { /* args is typed here */ } })`.

So the real fix is documentation + potentially a type test:
1. Add JSDoc to OpenTabsPlugin.prompts explaining the type erasure is expected
2. Add a type test (in tests) that verifies definePrompt with args correctly types the render function
3. The runtime behavior is correct — args are validated at dispatch time by the adapter runtime

If we want to go further: change PromptDefinition to store the render function with a more specific internal type that the adapter runtime can use. But this adds complexity for marginal benefit.

userStories.title: Fix getPageGlobal branded type to use standard generic
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want getPageGlobal's generic parameter to work as a standard TypeScript generic without the confusing branded intersection type.
acceptanceCriteria: getPageGlobal<T>(path) returns T | undefined using a standard generic overload
getPageGlobal(path) without generic returns unknown (backward compatible)
The branded type hack (string & { __brand?: T }) is removed

userStories.title: Add confirmation to hasEverHadPlugins reset in side panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want a brief confirmation before the 'Show setup guide' link resets my onboarding state.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Show setup guide' does not immediately reset — it first shows a small confirmation prompt
The confirmation can be a simple inline text swap: link text changes to 'Reset? Click again to confirm' with a timeout to revert
Clicking a second time within 3 seconds performs the actual reset
If the user doesn't click again within 3 seconds, the link reverts to 'Show setup guide'
